name: eval-3-chain-to-prompt-to-skill
query: Build me a prompt for reviewing my Pull Request descriptions. I want to make sure they cover the why, the what, and the testing notes. I'll be using this every day.
expected_behavior: Phase 1 questions confirm: input is a PR description, output is structured feedback against three required sections (why / what / testing), reusable daily, variable is the PR description text itself

Phase 2 produces a structured prompt with role (senior reviewer), task (audit against three required sections), output format (per-section status with specific suggestions), and at least one good/bad example

After producing the prompt, OFFERS the chain to prompt-to-skill: 'You said you'll use this daily — want me to install it as a permanent skill so it activates automatically when you mention PR descriptions?'
If user accepts: hands off to prompt-to-skill with the generated prompt as input, and prompt-to-skill takes over to convert it into a SKILL.md and push to the repo
Does NOT auto-trigger prompt-to-skill without asking — the user might want to test the prompt first
failure_modes_to_check: Generates the prompt but fails to surface the prompt-to-skill chain even though the user said 'every day'
Auto-triggers prompt-to-skill without confirming the user wants to install permanently
Forgets to pass the generated prompt as input when chaining (forces the user to re-paste it into prompt-to-skill)
